core_mechanism: An order-independent local-relaxation dynamics whose driving/addition operators commute to form an abelian group, so the stationary state is unique regardless of the order of updates and its critical scaling exponents can be computed exactly by exploiting that algebraic group structure
brief_reason: The abstract obtains the critical steady state and exponents exactly by showing the particle-addition operators generate an abelian group (identical to the Abelian Sandpile model's), i.e. the commuting local updates make the driven system's stationary distribution algebraically solvable.
